channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Ongoing Work of Transformation in South Africa
item.description: Meaningful transformation in South Africa is often misunderstood as a quick fix or a single event, yet the reality is far more complex. Over thirty years into democracy, the country still faces deep s...

item.description: On the eve of the scheduled hearing for the £190 million corruption case, the lawyers representing former Prime Minister Imran Khan and his spouse Bushra Bibi informed the Islamabad High Court (IHC) t...

item.title: NCC leader Fadiel Adams set to appear in Pinetown court amid fraud and investigation interference charges
item.description: National Coloured Congress (NCC) leader and MP Fadiel Adams, who was arrested in the Western Cape on Tuesday, is on route to Durban and is scheduled to appear in the Pinetown magistrate’s court on Thu...

item.title: Christopher Nolan’s Odyssey: analysis of its fidelity to Homer’s epic poem
item.description: Christopher Nolan’s forthcoming adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey, starring Matt Damon as Odysseus, has generated significant excitement with the release of a new trailer and a rare interview on Stephen C...

item.title: Tanzanian Rally Drivers Aim for Good Points in Uganda ARC Round
item.description: Tanzanian rally drivers are set to compete in the Pearl of Africa Uganda Rally, which runs from Wednesday to Saturday in Mbarara. The team is led by experienced driver Ahmed Huwel, who returns to the ...

item.title: Iran Signals Strait of Hormuz Could Reopen as US Pauses Military Operation
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East remain high as Iran has announced that the Strait of Hormuz could reopen following the pause of the US 'Project Freedom'. The Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) state...

item.title: Single Motorist Critically Injured in Caledon Collision
item.description: A serious traffic incident occurred in Caledon, within the Peel region, leaving one individual in critical condition. The crash took place on May 6, 2026, and emergency services responded promptly to ...

item.title: Internal trade barriers hinder Canadian alcohol sales across provinces
item.description: Internal trade barriers between Canadian provinces continue to limit the sale and distribution of domestic alcohol, particularly wine. Robbie Raskin, owner of Archives Wine and Spirit Merchants, highl...

item.title: Tanzania and Kenya Strengthen Ties for Regional Economic Growth
item.description: The recent visit of Kenyan President William Ruto to Tanzania, highlighted by his address to the Tanzanian Parliament, marks a significant step in improving bilateral relations. Both nations are worki...

item.title: JD Vance stumbles over notes during Iowa speech while campaigning for Republican colleague
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance faced online mockery after an awkward moment during a speech in Des Moines, Iowa, on Tuesday. While addressing a manufacturing facility and campaigning for GOP Representativ...

item.title: Wike Clarifies He Has Not Endorsed Candidate for Rivers Governorship, Confirms Rift With Fubara
item.description: The Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, Nyesom Wike, don yan say he never endorse anybody for the 2027 Rivers State governorship election. Wike talk this one during him monthly media chat for A...

item.title: Loblaw's Profits Rise Amid Expansion of Discount Store Network
item.description: Loblaw Cos. Ltd., Canada’s largest grocery retailer based in Brampton, Ontario, reported a 6.8-per-cent increase in adjusted first-quarter profit, reflecting strong performance from its discount store...

item.title: Leandro Leviste denies allegations and invites Ralph Recto to clarify before Congress
item.description: Batangas 1st District Representative Leandro Leviste has firmly denied claims made by Executive Secretary Ralph Recto that he offered P1 billion for Recto’s wife, Vilma Santos-Recto, not to run in the...

item.title: Ramaphosa and Mozambican President Reject Xenophobia Label for South Africa During Bilateral Talks
item.description: Presidency spokesperson Vincent Magwenya has firmly rejected blanket characterisations of South Africa as a xenophobic country. Speaking at a media briefing in Cape Town, Magwenya emphasised that Pres...

item.title: US set to lift Eritrea sanctions amid Red Sea strategic shifts
item.description: The United States is planning to lift sanctions on Eritrea, a move linked to the country's strategic position along the Red Sea, which is now more important because of the ongoing war in Iran and the ...

item.title: Sowore Leads Protest Against MTN Over Expensive Data and Poor Service Quality
item.description: For Abuja, on Wednesday, Omoyele Sowore, the former presidential candidate and human rights activist, lead angry Nigerians to protest in front of MTN Nigeria head office for Maitama. The protesters de...

item.title: US and Iran reportedly close to memorandum of understanding to end conflict in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump has stated that Operation Epic Fury will end if Iran agrees to a proposed peace deal, but warned that bombing would resume at a significantly higher level and intensity if Te...

item.title: Standard Chartered to sell Nairobi East Africa headquarters as part of global restructuring
item.description: Standard Chartered Bank imeamua kuuza ofisi yake kuu ya East Africa iliyoko Nairobi, hatua hii ni sehemu ya mpango mkubwa wa benki hiyo kupunguza gharama na kuongeza ufanisi. Ofisi ya Chiromo Road, ye...

item.title: Tim Tszyu relocates to Miami training hub with Mike Tyson influence amid comeback plans
item.description: Australian boxer Tim Tszyu has relocated his training base to Miami, with boxing legend Mike Tyson playing a key role in fuelling the move. The former world champion is eyeing a strong return to the r...
